routes.title: About
routes.description: Coder is a self-hosted platform for running AI coding agents and cloud development environments on the infrastructure you control.

children.title: Screenshots
children.description: See screenshots of the Coder platform, including browser-based workspace access and the admin dashboard.

children.title: Support
children.description: Get help with your Coder deployment through GitHub issues, our Discord community, and premium support.

children.title: Generate a Support Bundle
children.description: Generate a support bundle and share workspace and deployment diagnostics with Coder support to troubleshoot issues.

children.title: Contributing
children.description: Set up your development environment and learn the workflow for contributing code to Coder.

children.title: Code of Conduct
children.description: Read the Contributor Covenant code of conduct that governs participation in the Coder community.

children.title: Documentation
children.description: Follow Coder's style guide conventions for voice, formatting, and terminology when you author documentation.

children.title: Modules
children.description: Learn how to build and contribute reusable Terraform modules to the Coder Registry.

children.title: Templates
children.description: Learn how to build and contribute complete workspace templates to the Coder Registry.

children.title: Backend
children.description: A guide to Coder's backend systems to help engineers and community contributors start backend development.

children.title: Frontend
children.description: A guide to Coder's frontend codebase and conventions to help you start contributing to the UI.

children.title: Security
children.description: Read Coder's security policy and learn how to responsibly report a security vulnerability.

children.title: AI Contribution Guidelines
children.description: Follow the rules for pull requests where an AI system is the primary author of the code.

routes.title: Get started
routes.description: Install Coder, build your first template, and launch your first workspace in this quickstart guide.

children.title: Customize your template
children.description: Add a language, install command-line tools, and authenticate to GitHub

children.title: Add a programming language
children.description: Expose a new programming language through a template parameter and install it at workspace startup.

children.title: Install your own command-line tools
children.description: Install your own command-line tools in a workspace and make them persist across rebuilds.

children.title: Clone private repositories
children.description: Clone private GitHub repositories in your workspace using a Coder external-auth data source.

routes.title: Install
routes.description: Install the Coder server and CLI, then choose a deployment method for your infrastructure.

children.title: Coder CLI
children.description: Install the standalone Coder CLI binary on Linux, macOS, or Windows to run the server and client.

children.title: Docker
children.description: Install and run Coder using the official Docker images for a quick single-machine deployment.

children.title: Kubernetes
children.description: Install Coder on a Kubernetes cluster using the official Helm chart for production deployments.

children.title: Deploy Coder on Azure with an Application Gateway
children.description: Deploy Coder on Azure Kubernetes Service behind an Azure Application Gateway ingress controller.

children.title: Rancher
children.description: Deploy Coder on Rancher as a Kubernetes workload using the Rancher UI and Helm.

children.title: OpenShift
children.description: Install Coder on a Red Hat OpenShift cluster using Helm, with the required security context settings.

children.title: Cloud Providers
children.description: Install guides and example templates for deploying Coder on AWS, Google Cloud, and Azure.

children.title: AWS Marketplace
children.description: Deploy a Coder proof-of-concept on AWS using the listing available in the AWS Marketplace.

children.title: GCP Compute Engine
children.description: Deploy the Coder control plane on a Google Cloud Compute Engine virtual machine.

children.title: Azure VM
children.description: Set up the Coder server on a Microsoft Azure virtual machine, step by step.

children.title: Air-gapped Deployments
children.description: Run Coder in air-gapped, disconnected, or offline environments where all features remain supported.

children.title: Unofficial Install Methods
children.description: Explore community-contributed, unofficial methods for installing Coder beyond the supported paths.

children.title: Upgrading
children.description: Learn how to upgrade your Coder server to a newer version, with steps for each install method.

children.title: Upgrading Best Practices
children.description: Follow best practices and troubleshooting tips to plan and carry out Coder upgrades.

children.title: Uninstall
children.description: Learn how to uninstall your Coder server and remove its associated resources.

children.title: Releases
children.description: Learn about Coder's release channels, versioning, and schedule so you can plan upgrades.

children.title: Feature stages
children.description: Understand Coder's feature stages, including early access, beta, and general availability.

children.title: Upgrading from ESR 2.24 to 2.29
children.description: A step-by-step guide to upgrade a Coder deployment from Extended Support Release 2.24 to 2.29.

children.title: Upgrading from ESR 2.29 to 2.34
children.description: A step-by-step guide to upgrade a Coder deployment from Extended Support Release 2.29 to 2.34.

routes.title: User Guides
routes.description: Guides for Coder end users on accessing, managing, and personalizing their workspaces.

children.title: Access Workspaces
children.description: Connect to your Coder workspace using SSH, your IDE, a web terminal, remote desktop, and more.

children.title: Visual Studio Code
children.description: Develop in your Coder workspace remotely with VS Code, using the desktop app or the browser.

children.title: Web Terminal
children.description: Use the browser-based web terminal to run commands in your workspace without any local setup.

children.title: JetBrains IDEs
children.description: Connect JetBrains IDEs to Coder workspaces using JetBrains Toolbox or Gateway.

children.title: JetBrains Fleet
children.description: Connect JetBrains Fleet, a lightweight multi-language IDE, to a Coder workspace over SSH.

children.title: JetBrains Gateway
children.description: Use JetBrains Gateway to open and work in your Coder workspace from a full JetBrains IDE.

children.title: JetBrains Toolbox
children.description: Connect to and manage your Coder workspaces from the JetBrains Toolbox app.

children.title: Remote Desktop
children.description: Connect to a GUI-based desktop in your Coder workspace using RDP, including Windows workspaces.

children.title: Emacs TRAMP
children.description: Use Emacs TRAMP to edit files and run commands in your Coder workspace over SSH.

children.title: Port Forwarding
children.description: Securely access ports and processes running inside your Coder workspace from your local machine.

children.title: Filebrowser
children.description: Upload, download, and manage files in your Coder workspace from the browser with File Browser.

children.title: Web IDEs and Coder Apps
children.description: Access your Coder workspace through web-based IDEs that run in the browser, with no local install.

children.title: code-server
children.description: Access your Coder workspace in the browser with code-server, the VS Code experience over the web.

children.title: Zed
children.description: Access your Coder workspace with the Zed collaborative code editor over SSH.

children.title: Cursor
children.description: Access your Coder workspace with Cursor, the AI-powered code editor, over SSH.

children.title: Windsurf
children.description: Access your Coder workspace with Windsurf, the AI-powered code editor, over SSH.

children.title: Antigravity
children.description: Access your Coder workspace with the Antigravity agentic code editor over SSH.

children.title: Coder Desktop
children.description: Transform remote workspaces into seamless local development environments with no port forwarding required

children.title: Coder Desktop Connect and Sync
children.description: Use Coder Desktop to connect to workspaces and sync their code and files to your local machine.

children.title: Workspace Management
children.description: Create, start, stop, update, and delete your Coder workspaces from the dashboard or CLI.

children.title: Workspace Sharing
children.description: Let multiple users securely connect to a single Coder workspace to program and debug together.

children.title: Workspace Scheduling
children.description: Control cloud costs by scheduling when your Coder workspaces automatically start and stop.

children.title: Workspace Lifecycle
children.description: A guide to the workspace lifecycle, from creation and status through stopping and deletion.

children.title: Dev Containers
children.description: Run containerized development environments in your Coder workspace using the dev containers specification.

children.title: Working with dev containers
children.description: Access and work inside dev containers in your Coder workspace over SSH, your IDE, or the web terminal.

children.title: Customizing dev containers
children.description: Configure custom agent names, apps, and display options in devcontainer.json.

children.title: Troubleshooting dev containers
children.description: Diagnose and resolve common issues with dev containers in your Coder workspace.

children.title: Dotfiles
children.description: Personalize your Coder workspaces by applying your dotfiles repository with the coder dotfiles command.

children.title: User secrets
children.description: Store secret values in Coder and automatically inject them into workspaces

routes.title: Administration
routes.description: Guides for the template and deployment administrators who configure, secure, and operate Coder.

children.title: Appearance

children.description: Learn what usage telemetry Coder collects, why it is collected, and how to opt out.

children.title: Data Retention

children.description: Learn how to plan, provision, and manage the infrastructure that runs your Coder deployment.

children.title: Architecture

children.title: Validated Architectures
children.description: Coder Validated Architectures with reference designs and best practices for large deployments.

children.description: Use Coder's built-in scale-testing utility to benchmark and load-test your deployment.

children.description: Best practices for preparing and tuning a Coder deployment so it scales to many users.

children.description: Inject environment variables into Coder workspaces using the coder_env Terraform resource.

children.description: Authenticate the Coder server to cloud provider APIs so it can provision workspaces.

children.description: Add an Open in Coder button to your repositories and wikis so users can create workspaces fast.

routes.title: Run AI Coding Agents in Coder
routes.description: Run and manage AI coding agents like Claude Code and Codex in Coder workspaces, alongside your existing development environments.

children.title: Best Practices
children.description: Cultural and technical best practices for introducing AI coding agents into your organization.

children.title: In the IDE
children.description: Run in-IDE AI coding assistants and agents while backing their compute with Coder workspaces.

children.description: Delegate development work to coding agents in your deployment with Coder Agents, a chat interface and API.

children.title: Getting Started
children.description: Enable Coder Agents, prepare your deployment, and run your first Coder Agent

children.title: Search Syntax
children.description: Filter Coder Agents conversations by title, status, and linked pull requests with search syntax.

children.description: Learn how the Coder Agents control-plane agent communicates with your workspaces.
